scope_boundary: synthetic governance-right record discipline and finite Lean governance-right predicates only; no institutional governance right, legal right, deployed runtime enforcement, fork/exit usability, reviewer independence, source interpretation, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has a governance-right schema, a protocol fixture, finite GovernanceRights Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps the target narrow, exact source mappings for the Governance Rights chapter, and a synthetic governance-right harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make audit material and receipts, redaction appeal paths, materially usable exit and fork access paths, fork safety constraints, preservation obligations, durable record paths, and explicit non-claim boundaries executable at the record level. They still do not establish institutional governance rights, legal rights, deployed runtime right enforcement, real fork or exit usability, reviewer independence, source interpretation adequacy, or whole-system contestability.
required_artifacts: deployed governance-right request flow with audit, exit, fork, redaction, appeal, and dissent records
material-usability review over actual exported or forkable artifacts
runtime enforcement trace showing rights preservation across replacement or policy update
reviewer-independence audit for challenged-party governance decisions
safety-bound fork scenario with actual source, privacy, and capability constraints preserved
independent legal, governance, or source-interpretation review where legal or institutional language is used
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_governance_rights.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: schema validation checks record shape rather than institutional governance
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for legal, institutional, or deployed-right enforcement claims
material usability, reviewer independence, runtime enforcement, fork safety in real systems, legal adequacy, and source interpretation review are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as legal-rights evidence
synthetic access-path fixture interpreted as deployed fork or exit usability
synthetic appeal fixture interpreted as reviewer independence or institutional appeal quality
finite Lean audit/exit predicates treated as deployed runtime enforcement
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after deployed governance-right flows, material-usability review, runtime preservation traces, reviewer-independence audit, safety-bound fork scenarios, and independent legal, governance, or source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove that real users can audit, exit, fork, appeal, or preserve dissent
schema validation does not prove legal, institutional, or governance adequacy
finite Lean predicates do not implement a governance-right service
access-path strings do not prove that exported or forkable artifacts are materially usable
appeal-path strings do not prove reviewer independence or appeal quality
no independent legal, governance, runtime, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent governance, legal, runtime-systems, reviewer-quality, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no material-usability review over actual exports or forks
no safety-bound fork scenario over real artifacts
no independent legal, governance, runtime, or source-interpretation review
non_claims: does not promote Governance Rights above argument
does not prove institutional governance rights
does not prove deployed runtime right enforcement
does not prove fork or exit usability in a real system
does not prove reviewer independence or source interpretation
